title: Pandaca humblotii
intro: Pandaca humblotii, also known as Madagascar periwinkle, is a flowering plant species native to the island of Madagascar. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer properties, as well as the potential to regulate blood sugar levels. The bioactive constituents of Pandaca humblotii include alkaloids such as vincristine and vinblastine, which have been isolated and used in chemotherapy. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include extracts, capsules, and infusions, often used to treat conditions such as diabetes, arthritis, and certain types of cancer.
